content.thinking:
Let me analyze the three grids:

Target grid:
- Has a vertical block of 3 X's in the center (columns 2, 3, 4)
- Empty cells on both left and right sides (columns 1 and 5)
- All 5 rows have the same pattern

Distractor grid 1:
- Has a vertical block of 3 X's on the right side (columns 3, 4, 5)
- Empty cells on the left side (columns 1 and 2)
- All 5 rows have the same pattern

Distractor grid 2:
- Has a vertical block of 3 X's on the left side (columns 1, 2, 3)
- Empty cells on the right side (columns 4 and 5)
- All 5 rows have the same pattern

The key distinguishing feature of the target grid is that the three filled columns are centered, with one empty column on each side. This is different from the distractors where the filled columns are aligned to one side.

I could describe it as:
- "Three vertical columns of X's centered with empty columns on both sides"
- "A centered vertical stripe of three columns"
- "Three filled columns in the middle flanked by empty columns on left and right"

content.text: Expression: Three vertical columns filled with X in the center, with one empty column on each side
